title: barringtonia demissa
intro: Barringtonia demissa, also known as the Putat tree, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Barringtonia demissa include its potential to treat skin conditions, wounds, and infections, as well as its ability to lower fever and alleviate pain. Barringtonia demissa can be prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
